"God is on the side of the heaviest slop cannons."
- Napoleon Bonaparte

This past weekend, I went to my first hackathon, Slop Con, hosted by @yrechtman and @Jack_Raines of @slow.

The void graciously accepted my salvos of tokens. https://t.co/9dvtvWsnLg https://t.co/fz2yCIXfIO
only on X is this the greatest praise one's work can receive https://t.co/ad1DV15Uqp
@Jack_Raines The only worthwhile new podcast episodes feature exceptionally talented guests who are rarely, if ever, interviewed.

@ryanbrewer thank you for the madden mobile reminder.

pure nostalgia looking back to when that first dropped.

@signulll It's pretty much impossible to describe to anyone how hard something was to accomplish.

Any extra effort to persuade others makes it seem less impressive.

@ns123abc Both things can be true, rate limits have been an issue with anthropic.

The Information’s bias towards OAI is real tho.

@johncoogan @tbpn @sama Congratulations!!!

I know you will try your best to stay independent. But most viewers being a media company owned by any corporation leads to natural skepticism.

I am confident the show’s quality will skyrocket.

I am just surprised bc it was trending t

@axepreneur use research as a drunk uses a lamppost

not for illumination

but for support

- david ogilvy

@davidsenra @elonmusk Funny how Musk has delusional levels of optimism and avoids wishful thinking. 

Yet it makes perfect sense.
@brian_armstrong Descending from the local peaks is the hardest part for most people when working on something ambitious.

@robmcgrorty @FoundersPodcast Haha that rules man. 0.5% is next level.

Last year I was inconsistent.

I try to stay actively engaged with every episode.

I only listen when I can really focus, so once a day is good for me.

Not sure where I will land this year.

@robmcgrorty @davidsenra @FoundersPodcast Robert Moses was arguably more influential than any NYC mayor of the 20th century, for better or worse.

I completely agree that it would make a fantastic episode.

Random repost by the cofounder of @eightsleep to my unrelated reply to a david senra post from a day ago

I love this app

Here is what I said btw. The last sentence is my favorite. https://t.co/okOmCA0goC https://t.co/GP5rpSnfwe

@SpiceP0dcast @FoundersPodcast Thank you, Alon 🙏

Excited for the future you're creating with Spice.

@davidsenra @brian_armstrong The best prize that life has to offer is the chance to work hard at work worth doing.

- Teddy Roosevelt
@blakeir The bright side of the proliferation of AI-slop web design is that it has elevated the level of creativity required to stand out.

@arudansk @FoundersPodcast Terrific list. 

Although not from his shareholder letters, Bezos’s regret-minimization framework (which ultimately led to his decision to found Amazon) is a personal favorite idea from all of Founders Podcast.

@blakeir The quality has skyrocketed. 

The search cost has shifted from “where can I find quality content?” to “what is worth my time?”

@FoundersPodcast Leave more on the table for the other guy than he thinks he should get. - Ken Langone

I’ve found that this mindset helps surface advantages you’d otherwise miss when things get heated.

@MEGA_Creativity every great victory is nothing more than the starting point for the next one

- enzo ferrari

@WillManidis Strong parallels to the 1890s right now. If you break down how railroads unlocked our economy, it’s not much different than AI. The new tycoons are laying down the tracks with data centers. 

There are other reasons too, but it's easy to argue that any era is similar
@patrick_oshag "If you're following your calling, the fatigue will be easier to bear. The disappointments will be fuel. The highs will be like nothing you've ever felt." - Phil Knight

@johncoogan If 95% of Moltbot users adopt it for email automation and a few admin tasks, this won’t move the needle. People are doing cool things with it no doubt about it. That said, Moltbot reminds me of the initial surge in hype and subsequent cooldown with agentic browsers. N
@FoundersPodcast The video of Rubin recording 99 problems with Jay is one of the coolest studio moments captured on camera in hip hop history. Rubin’s insight to start the song accapella was genius.

@andrew__reed I have a very open-minded relative in his 80s, and he can hold insightful conversations about the latest technology topics (e.g., AI). He’s brought my family and me many rich memories at the table over the years, simply by trying and being willing to learn new thing
@johncoogan Studying founders raised in the Great Depression is the best resource for this approach
@davidsenra Sam Hinkie interview?? His story needs to be told.
@rookisaacman We need our Henry Kaiser 2.0
@FoundersPodcast Favorite interview of 2025
@KennethCassel 1925 number really stands out. Assuming the rates were measured several years out I’d guess some of it is Depression driven. Hard to stay healthy when you can barely remain satiated.
@DavidJeans2 To be fair if budget spend for defense tech almost doubled y/y, it’s almost a given manufacturing is not scaled
@kingofstrato @paulg @ycombinator @garrytan Favorite @tbpn interview of YC demo day. Prototype looks great!

@BucknSF If an ipo is inevitable, it’s a smart way to beat out OpenAI in the financial services industry. Much less significant enterprise SaaS vendors push around middle-market banks all the time. I imagine Anthropic will have everyone and a 4% gross spread.

@CarlChiou @BucknSF
@jacksondahl Senra is like jet fuel for focusing on the goal. That would not be possible without his passion.
